title: Domain Node
description: Top-level technology area with no parent (0 dots in ID). Domains group related capabilities sharing >50% infrastructure, knowledge, and practitioner overlap (SIK test).

@id.description: JSON-LD node IRI. Alias for id via @context mapping.

id.description: Unique identifier: single kebab-case segment with no dots (0 dots = domain).

description.description: 1-3 sentence summary of what this node represents and why it matters.

timeline.description: Estimated time range as a free-text string (e.g., 'Years 0-10').

outputs.description: Product/material/capability tokens produced by this node.

critical.description: True if this node is a critical-path enabler.

critical_note.description: Why this node is critical. Only present when critical=true.

early_win.description: True if this node provides a high-value early output creating a positive feedback loop.

early_win_note.description: Description of the feedback loop. Only present when early_win=true.

pinnacle.description: True if this node is an end-goal requiring a mature ecosystem.

pinnacle_note.description: Why this is a pinnacle achievement. Only present when pinnacle=true.

taxonomy.description: Optional hierarchical material-origin classification using dotted kebab-case strings. First segment must be one of: biomass, mineral, synthetic.

wikidataId.description: Wikidata entity identifier linking this node to the corresponding Wikidata item (e.g., 'Q880' for iron).

tags.description: Classification tags along material, capability, era, and lifecycle dimensions.

material.description: Primary physical substances produced or transformed. 0-3 values from closed vocabulary.

capability.description: Primary functional capabilities. Domain nodes may inherit the union of children's tags.

era.description: Earliest historical era this node becomes achievable. Exactly one value.

lifecycle.description: Optional material lifecycle role tags. 0-5 values from closed vocabulary.
